summary:
The only doc I edited is `docs/developer-guide.md` (the `input.json` change was already present at session start).

## Summary

The developer guide already carried a §5 subsection, "Adding a derived, live-broadcast status view," but it had been written against an earlier (iteration-1) version of this design and had drifted from the settled iteration-2 technical design. Rather than add redundant content, I reconciled that subsection to match the final design while keeping its existing three-piece (read / render / broadcast) structure and tone. Specifically: the read is now correctly described as a domain **value PORO** at `app/lib/pipelines/status_summary.rb` (not a query object in `app/queries`), entered via `Pipelines::StatusSummary.for(pipeline)` (`.for`, not `.call`), returning `Summary = Data.define(:text, :tone, :phase_label)` from a **total** `build` with an ordered first-match branch list, active-run thresholds (1 names it / 2 names both / 3+ collapse to a count) and the iteration-only-shown-on-2nd+-pass rule; the render section adds the `compact:` two-variant partial, the new `pipelines/index` compact surface and its first `turbo_stream_from`, the `PipelinesHelper#summary_dot_class` helper, and the `Pipeline.with_board` preload scope; and the broadcast section corrects the service name to `Pipelines::BroadcastStatus`, fixes the call sites to Claim/RecordProgress/Complete/ManagerTick, explains the job-reload re-render that makes late broadcasts safe, and demotes `StepRuns::Sweep`/`Pipelines::Create`/a real `paused` status to explicitly-out-of-scope follow-ups. Illustrative examples in the intro were aligned to the final wording. No other docs needed changes, since the feature introduces no new models, endpoints, or migrations.
